title: asplenium trichomanes subsp. coriaceifolium
intro: Asplenium trichomanes subsp. coriaceifolium, also known as the Coriaceous-leaf Maidenhair Spleenwort, is a subspecies of the Maidenhair Spleenwort, a type of fern native to Europe and parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Asplenium trichomanes subsp. coriaceifolium include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant is typically harvested and dried for use in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
